sources.label: NHS summary list
borrowed: Use summary-list structure for concise reviewable appointment facts.
Keep row actions contextual instead of relying on isolated labels.
rejected: Turning every artifact action into an inline row action that competes with the receipt summary.

sources.label: NHS how we write
borrowed: Use factual, neutral, unambiguous wording.
Say what the current posture is without optimistic reassurance.
rejected: Decorative or certificate-like language for the appointment receipt.

sources.label: NHS PDFs and non-HTML documents
borrowed: Keep structured HTML as the primary reading surface.
Treat non-HTML delivery as secondary and potentially constrained.
rejected: A PDF-first or print-first booking artifact journey.

sources.label: NHS buttons
borrowed: Use buttons for transactional actions inside the journey.
Keep one clear dominant action and full-width mobile targets.
rejected: Link-styled artifact buttons that imply a detached browser page.

sources.label: WCAG 2.2 status messages
borrowed: Programmatically expose status changes without moving focus.
rejected: Mode changes that only update the screen visually.

sources.label: WCAG 2.4.3 focus order
borrowed: Keep focus order aligned with the receipt-first reading sequence.
rejected: Parity rail or support controls appearing before the summary in keyboard order.

sources.label: WAI alert pattern
borrowed: Do not move focus for brief important status updates.
rejected: Interruptive alert dialogs for ordinary mode changes.

sources.label: WAI modal dialog pattern
borrowed: Return focus to the invoking control when a modal closes.
rejected: Detached overlays for print or browser handoff that lose the invoking context.

sources.label: Playwright aria snapshots
borrowed: Capture aria snapshots for the artifact frame rather than only screenshot evidence.
rejected: Visual-only proof for parity-sensitive artifact states.

sources.label: Playwright accessibility testing
borrowed: Combine automated checks with explicit structural assertions.
rejected: Treating automated accessibility checks as the only source of truth.

sources.label: Playwright visual comparisons
borrowed: Keep visual proof deterministic with captured screenshots.
rejected: Ad hoc manual screenshots with no route-level contract coverage.

sources.label: Playwright trace viewer
borrowed: Capture traces for route, mode, and return-safe behavior.
rejected: Debugging mode transitions without replayable traces.

sources.label: Playwright emulateMedia
borrowed: Use print media emulation to prove the print surface keeps the same meaning.
rejected: Inferring print parity from CSS inspection alone.

sources.label: Carbon read-only states
borrowed: Keep read-only information readable while removing misleading interaction cues.
rejected: Disabled-looking artifact states that fail contrast or screen-reader readability.

sources.label: Carbon status indicators
borrowed: Use plain text when extra status emphasis is unnecessary.
Reserve stronger status treatments for real user-action thresholds.
rejected: Overloading the artifact frame with too many decorative status chips.
